title: Pile
description: Pile: A pile object defined by it's location and section.

TopNode.description: Defines the top Node. Note that Nodes can contain Supports. Node: 0D finite element for structural analysis. Node class contains positional information as well as orientation and support.

BottomNode.description: Defines the end position of the Pile. Note that Nodes can contain Supports. Node: 0D finite element for structural analysis. Node class contains positional information as well as orientation and support.

Section.description: Section property of the Pile, containing all sectional constants and material as well as profile geometry and dimensions, where applicable. ISectionProperty: Base interface for all bar section properties. Contains the material and all section constants.

OrientationAngle.description:
Controls the local axis orientation of the Pile 
For non-vertical members the local z is aligned with the global Z and rotated with the orientation angle about the local x. 
For vertical members the local y is aligned with the global Y and rotated with the orientation angle about the local x. 
A Pile is vertical if its projected length to the horizontal plane is less than 0.0001, i.e. a tolerance of 0.1mm on verticality. 
For general structural conventions please see the documentation. Property has a quantity of type Angle measured in [rad].

_bhomVersion.description: Optional version of BHoM used as part of automatic versioning and schema upgrades.
